# Piper TTS — local paths (this machine / this repo)
# Generated 2026-04-02. Models are large; keep under vendor/piper/models/ or symlink.

## Voice model (rhasspy/piper-voices, English US — good default for integration tests)
# ONNX + JSON must stay together.
MODEL_ONNX=/Users/sakib/Project/AI Video Generate/vendor/piper/models/en_US-lessac-medium/en_US-lessac-medium.onnx
MODEL_JSON=/Users/sakib/Project/AI Video Generate/vendor/piper/models/en_US-lessac-medium/en_US-lessac-medium.onnx.json

## Legacy Rhasspy CLI binary (release 2023.11.14-2)
# NOTE: piper_macos_aarch64.tar.gz currently ships an x86_64 binary. On Apple Silicon you can:
# - Prefer the Python API (recommended): piper package in .venv — see below, or
# - Run the CLI under Rosetta and point DYLD at an x86_64 libespeak-ng (fragile).
PIPER_CLI=/Users/sakib/Project/AI Video Generate/vendor/piper/dist/piper
ESPEAK_NG_DATA=/Users/sakib/Project/AI Video Generate/vendor/piper/dist/espeak-ng-data

## Python (OHF piper-tts, native arm64) — verified working with MODEL_ONNX
# import piper; PiperVoice.load(Path(MODEL_ONNX))
VENV_PYTHON=/Users/sakib/Project/AI Video Generate/.venv/bin/python

## Language note for BD/JP pipeline
# rhasspy/piper-voices has no ja_JP or bn_BD packs in the public index. For Bangla/Japanese you still
# need Gemini/OpenAI TTS, another engine, or a community ONNX if you find one.
